title: Remove a Robot From the Robot Selection Screen
summary: Use the Robot Selection screen in the web application or Geek Plus RMS view to remove a robot by entering or selecting its robot ID, clicking Remove, and confirming the system reports successful removal.
when_to_use: Use this procedure when support personnel need to remove a robot from the Robot Selection screen in the web application or Geek Plus RMS view during troubleshooting or support-related robot management, using an OT-network-connected device or authorized remote access as described in the source.
not_for: Do not use this procedure to decide whether a robot should be removed; the source states debug scenarios and when to remove the robot are covered later and are not included in this segment.

roles_and_responsibilities.responsibility: Access the Robot Selection screen, enter or select the robot ID, perform the Remove action, and verify the system reports successful removal.

safety_notes: Use only authorized access to the web application or Geek Plus RMS view.
The source does not provide approval criteria for when a robot should be removed; do not infer removal conditions from this segment alone.
access_or_tools_needed: Access to the web application or Geek Plus RMS view
Device connected to the OT network or authorized remote access through UPS VPN and Z Scaler
Robot ID for the robot being removed

access_required: Access to the web application or Geek Plus RMS view
OT-network-connected device, or authorized remote access through UPS VPN and Z Scaler
system_preconditions: The Robot Selection screen must be available in the web application or RMS view.
The user must know the robot ID or be able to select the robot from the Remove Robot area.

visual_references.description: Training slide showing the Robot Selection interface with Add Robot and Remove Robot procedures, including robot ID entry/selection and success confirmation.

steps.title: Open the Robot Selection screen
steps.purpose: Access the interface where the Remove Robot action is performed.
steps.instruction: Open the web application or Geek Plus RMS view that shows the Robot Selection screen. Use a device on the OT network, or authorized remote access if available according to the source.
steps.expected_result: The Robot Selection screen is available for use.
steps.healthy_condition: The user can access the web application or RMS view and navigate to the Robot Selection screen.
steps.failure_condition: The user cannot access the application, RMS view, or Robot Selection screen.

actions.label: Open Robot Selection
actions.exact_action: Open the web application or RMS view that shows the Robot Selection screen.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Robot Selection screen in the web application / RMS view.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if access to the OT-network-hosted application is unavailable and no authorized remote access is available.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Any device connected on the OT network, whether a laptop or computer, will have access to the web software application through that IP.
Remote support will not have access to OT net unless authorized to use the UPS VPN through Z Scaler; after authorization, the site address or RMS address can be used to access Geek Plus RMS.

steps.title: Locate the Remove Robot area
steps.purpose: Identify the correct section of the Robot Selection screen for robot removal.
steps.instruction: Locate the Remove Robot section on the Robot Selection screen.
steps.expected_result: The Remove Robot field and Remove button are visible.
steps.healthy_condition: The Remove Robot area is clearly visible and ready for robot ID entry or robot selection.
steps.failure_condition: The Remove Robot area is not visible or cannot be identified on the screen.

actions.label: Find Remove Robot section
actions.exact_action: Locate the Remove Robot section on the Robot Selection screen.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Screen view showing the Robot Selection area with the Remove Robot field and Remove button.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if the Robot Selection screen does not show the Remove Robot controls.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Slide shows Robot Selection with Add Robot and Remove Robot actions using robot ID entry and success confirmation.

steps.title: Enter or select the robot
steps.purpose: Identify the robot to be removed.
steps.instruction: Enter the corresponding robot ID or select the robot in the Remove Robot area.
steps.expected_result: The intended robot is selected in the Remove Robot workflow.
steps.healthy_condition: The correct robot ID is entered or the correct robot is selected.
steps.failure_condition: The robot cannot be found, selected, or confidently identified.

actions.label: Enter or select robot
actions.exact_action: Enter the corresponding robot ID or select the robot.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Robot ID entry area and robot selection behavior in the Remove Robot section.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if the correct robot cannot be identified or selected.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Remove Robot: 1. Enter the corresponding robot Id or select the robot.
Transcript notes typing the robot into the field below Robot and that partial ending digits can show matching robots if already introduced.

steps.title: Click Remove
steps.purpose: Execute the robot removal action in the interface.
steps.expected_result: The system processes the removal request.
steps.healthy_condition: The Remove action is accepted by the system.
steps.failure_condition: The Remove action cannot be initiated or does not process.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Remove button in the Remove Robot section.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if the Remove action is unavailable or does not execute.

steps.title: Verify successful removal
steps.purpose: Confirm the system reports that the removal completed successfully.
steps.instruction: Verify that the system shows the removal was successful.
steps.expected_result: The system displays a successful removal confirmation.
steps.healthy_condition: A success indication is shown for the removal action.
steps.failure_condition: The system does not show that removal was successful.

actions.label: Confirm success message
actions.exact_action: Verify that the system shows the removal was successful.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Success confirmation associated with the Remove Robot action.

stop_or_escalate_if: Escalate if the system does not show that removal was successful.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Remove Robot: 3. The system will show that the removal was successful.

steps.title: Use robot ID field matching behavior if needed
steps.purpose: Help locate an already introduced robot when the full robot ID is not manually entered first.
steps.instruction: If needed, use the robot ID field behavior described in training to find an already introduced robot by typing identifying digits and selecting a matching result when shown.
steps.expected_result: A matching robot appears for selection when the field returns existing robot matches.
steps.healthy_condition: Typing identifying digits surfaces the intended robot and allows selection.
steps.failure_condition: No matching robot appears or the intended robot cannot be confidently selected.

actions.label: Use partial ID matching
actions.exact_action: Type identifying digits in the robot field and select a matching result when shown.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Robot field used for ID entry and matching robot selection behavior.

stop_or_escalate_if: Stop or escalate if matching results do not allow confident identification of the intended robot.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Transcript notes typing the robot into the field below Robot and that partial ending digits can show matching robots if already introduced.

success_criteria: The intended robot is entered or selected in the Remove Robot area.
The Remove action is executed from the Robot Selection screen.
The system shows that the removal was successful.
healthy_conditions: The Robot Selection screen is accessible from the web application or Geek Plus RMS view.
The Remove Robot controls are visible.
The correct robot can be identified by robot ID entry or selection.
failure_conditions: The web application or RMS view cannot be accessed.
The Remove Robot section is not available.
The correct robot cannot be identified or selected.
The system does not show that removal was successful.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the system does not show that removal was successful.
Escalate if access to the application or Robot Selection screen is unavailable.
Escalate if the correct robot cannot be confidently identified for removal.
Do not escalate based on inferred removal criteria from this segment; the source says when to remove the robot is covered later.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Robot Selection screen with Remove Robot workflow, including robot ID entry/selection, Remove button, and success confirmation.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: Slide shows Robot Selection with Add Robot and Remove Robot actions using robot ID entry and success confirmation.
Remove Robot: 1. Enter the corresponding robot Id or select the robot. 2. Click "Remove". 3. The system will show that the removal was successful.

missing_details: The source segment does not define approval criteria or decision logic for when a robot should be removed.
The source does not provide a time estimate for completing this procedure.
The source does not specify whether production must be stopped before performing this action.
The source does not specify LOTO requirements for this action.
The source does not provide exact wording or location of the success message beyond stating that the system shows removal was successful.
